name: Miño de Medinaceli — circular via the Erm
name_original: Miño de Medinaceli — circular por la Ermita de la Virgen de la Santa Cruz
description: Hike the moderate 6.8-mile Miño de Medinaceli circular trail, exploring ancient ruins, geological formations, and the historic Ermita de la
region_intro: Hiking around Miño De Medinaceli offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, open countryside, and historical pathways. The region features a mix of agricultural land and natural areas, with moderate elevation changes suitable for various hiking abilities. Trails often connect small villages and historical sites, providing insight into the local heritage. The terrain is generally accessible, making it suitable for exploring the rural environment. Best hiking trails around Miño De Medinaceli The most popular hiking route is Medinaceli and Ambrona Loop, a 10.3 miles (16.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 43 minutes to complete, connecting two distinct areas with varied scenery. Another top favourite among local hikers is Medinaceli - Camino del Cid Trail, an easy 1.6 miles (2.5 km) path. This route follows a section of the historic Camino del Cid, offering a glimpse into the region's past. Local hikers also love the Yellow from Miño de Medinaceli — circular, a 5.9 miles (9.6 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Hiking around Miño De Medinaceli is defined by historical routes, open fields, and gentle hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from short, easy walks to longer, moderate excursions. The routes in Miño De Medinaceli are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 14 hikers have used komoot to explore Miño De Medinaceli's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Miño De Medinaceli?
A: There are over 14 hiking routes in the Miño De Medinaceli area, offering a variety of experiences from easy strolls to more moderate excursions through the countryside.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Miño De Medinaceli?
A: Yes, the region offers 7 easy trails perfect for beginners. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1263799305/medinaceli-camino-del-cid-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medinaceli - Camino del Cid Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 1.6-mile (2.5 km) path that follows a historic route.

Q: What are the typical lengths of hikes near Miño De Medinaceli?
A: Hikes in Miño De Medinaceli vary in length. You can find shorter paths like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1263799305/medinaceli-camino-del-cid-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medinaceli - Camino del Cid Trail&lt;/a&gt; at 1.6 miles (2.5 km), or longer routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059304105/medinaceli-and-ambrona-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medinaceli and Ambrona Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 10.3 miles (16.6 km).

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Miño De Medinaceli?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1263738098/yellow-from-mino-de-medinaceli-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yellow from Miño de Medinaceli — circular&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.9-mile (9.6 km) trail that takes you through the local countryside. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1263338437/mino-de-medinaceli-circular-via-the-erm" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miño de Medinaceli — circular via the Erm&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites or attractions can I see while hiking in the area?
A: The region is rich in history. You can explore sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2093474" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medinaceli&lt;/a&gt;, a historic settlement, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3967539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Humilladero&lt;/a&gt;. Many trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1263799305/medinaceli-camino-del-cid-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medinaceli - Camino del Cid Trail&lt;/a&gt;, follow ancient paths, offering a glimpse into the past.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or scenic viewpoints to discover?
A: Yes, the area features several natural points of interest. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5617038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pared&lt;/a&gt; natural monument or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7849684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de los Corrales de Sinova&lt;/a&gt; gorge. These offer scenic views and unique geological formations.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Miño De Medinaceli?
A: The trails in Miño De Medinaceli are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.0 stars. Hikers often appreciate the diverse landscape, historical pathways, and the peaceful rural environment.

Q: What is the best season for hiking around Miño De Medinaceli?
A: The diverse landscape of rolling hills and open countryside makes Miño De Medinaceli enjoyable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer can be warm but still suitable for early morning or late afternoon hikes.

Q: Are there any advanced or difficult hiking trails in Miño De Medinaceli?
A: The trails around Miño De Medinaceli are primarily rated as easy to moderate. There are no trails currently classified as difficult, making the area accessible for a wide range of hiking abilities looking for enjoyable, manageable excursions.

Q: Are there options for family-friendly walks in Miño De Medinaceli?
A: Yes, with 7 easy trails available, Miño De Medinaceli is well-suited for family walks. These shorter, less challenging paths allow families to enjoy the natural surroundings and historical sites together at a comfortable pace.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails?
A: The terrain around Miño De Medinaceli is characterized by rolling hills, open countryside, and agricultural land. Trails are generally accessible with moderate elevation changes, often connecting small villages and historical sites. You can expect a mix of dirt paths and rural tracks.

Q: Are there any hidden gem walks or less-known trails worth exploring?
A: While specific 'hidden gems' are often discovered by local exploration, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1263689905/romanillos-de-medinaceli-circular-por-la-maltida" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Romanillos de Medinaceli — circular por La Maltida&lt;/a&gt; offer a chance to explore the quieter parts of the region away from the most popular spots, providing a more secluded experience.
tips: Miño de Medinaceli is a small rural town in the province of Soria. You can park next to the community pools and start your walk. Climb up to see the ruins of a Moorish-occupied castle dating back to the 10th century and sitting on top of a hill above the city.
Walking along the rocky wall, shaped by the passing of time and climate. The Wall is an impressive geological landscape.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Miño de Medinaceli — circular by the Ermita de la Virgen de la Santa Cruz hike?
faqs.answer: This circular route usually takes around 2 hours and 49 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace and how much time you spend exploring the historical sites along the way.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features varied terrain, but the paths are generally easy to walk on. You can expect a mix of natural surfaces as you traverse the landscape around Miño de Medinaceli, including areas near the ruins and geological formations.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, hikers typically find parking in or near the village of Miño de Medinaceli, which serves as the starting point for this circular route. It's advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends, to secure a convenient spot.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in history and natural beauty. Key highlights include the ancient Ruins of Miño de Medinaceli, featuring a 10th-century Moorish castle. You'll also encounter the impressive geological formation known as La Pared, the 18th-century Ermita de la Virgen de la Santa Cruz, and the historically significant Cueva de la Santa Cruz with its prehistoric engravings.

faqs.question: Is the Miño de Medinaceli — circular by the Ermita de la Virgen de la Santa Cruz trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through historical sites or near wildlife, and to ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to visit are typically spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is usually mild and pleasant for hiking, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can be hot, and winter might bring colder temperatures, though the trail remains accessible.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Miño de Medinaceli — circular by the Ermita de la Virgen de la Santa Cruz trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What historical significance does the Cueva de la Santa Cruz hold?
faqs.answer: The Cueva de la Santa Cruz is a site of considerable historical and archaeological importance, dating back to the Bronze Age (2000-1600 BCE). It's known for its prehistoric engravings, including hundreds of cup-marks and schematic human figures, suggesting it was a sacred place for ancient rituals associated with a Great Mother Goddess. The hermitage was later built adjacent to it, overlaying pagan worship with Christian symbolism.

faqs.question: Is this a good trail for beginners?
faqs.answer: While classified as 'moderate,' the trail requires good basic fitness but no special skills. The paths are generally easy to walk on, making it accessible for beginners who are comfortable with a nearly 11 km distance and modest elevation changes.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or services available near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts near Miño de Medinaceli. While it's a small village, you might find basic services. For more extensive options like cafes, restaurants, or shops, you would likely need to visit the nearby larger town of Medinaceli.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, it's advisable to bring plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for the weather. A camera is also recommended to capture the historical sites and natural beauty.
